title: Secret Hidden Rooms Found Inside the Menkaure Pyramid!
summary:
Imagine you're an archaeologist, and you think there's a secret door hidden inside a giant pyramid. That's exactly what happened at the Menkaure Pyramid in Egypt! Scientists from Cairo University and the Technical University of Munich used special machines to look through the stone without breaking it. They used radar (like bats use to find their way) and ultrasound (like doctors use to see babies in tummies). And guess what? They found two hidden, air-filled rooms behind the eastern wall!

For years, experts noticed a section of the wall that looked super smooth and polished — just like the real entrance on the north side. They wondered if there was a second entrance there. Now, the scans show two voids (empty spaces) behind that smooth spot. One is about as tall as a 10-year-old kid and as wide as a door. The other is a bit smaller.

"The hypothesis of another entrance is very plausible," said Professor Christian Grosse. This means the secret entrance idea is looking more and more real. This is a huge discovery — like finding a hidden room in your own house that nobody knew about!
why_it_matters: It shows that even old famous places like pyramids can still surprise us, and that using science can help uncover hidden secrets.

keywords.explanation: A giant pyramid in Egypt, one of the three famous pyramids at Giza.
A machine that uses radio waves to see through things, like how bats see.
A machine that uses sound waves to see inside things, like doctors use on babies.
Empty spaces or rooms inside the pyramid.
A guess or idea that scientists want to test.
A door or opening to go inside.

questions.question: What did scientists use to see inside the pyramid?
How many hidden rooms did they find?
Where were the hidden rooms located?
How tall is the larger void compared to?
Who said the hypothesis of another entrance is plausible?

background_read: Pyramids were built as tombs for pharaohs over 4,000 years ago.
Scientists have used similar scanning methods to find hidden rooms in other pyramids, like the Great Pyramid in 2023.
The Menkaure Pyramid is the smallest of the three famous pyramids at Giza in Egypt.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from Cairo University and the Technical University of Munich
WHAT: They used radar and ultrasound to find two hidden air-filled rooms behind the eastern wall of the Menkaure Pyramid.
WHERE: In the Menkaure Pyramid in Egypt, specifically behind the eastern wall.
WHY: Because they noticed a smooth, polished section of the wall that might be a second entrance like the one on the north side.

perspectives.description: They are excited because new scanning technology can find hidden rooms without damaging the pyramid.
They might be thrilled to know there could be a secret entrance to explore, but it may take years to open.
They are interested in what the rooms might contain, but they worry about preserving the pyramid.
